summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics — the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (⇒ 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (⇒ 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence — never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (59) sits under a doc (55-58) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' — a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (630) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (657), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts — hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it — 528 ('hop-capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`]') and 626-629 ('capped at [`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`] hops (the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents)') — carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 652-655 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth'). 6667265 (mention-reachable, in the b85df4a..HEAD window) shifted every address after ~519 — the const (59) and its doc (55-58) sit above the edit and are unmoved; the later addresses re-read at HEAD, so every address carries.

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1407) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment — `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead as the sole finding — so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1408-1412) spells 'capped at the format's five-hop import recursion' (1410) and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number. 6667265 (mention-reachable, in the b85df4a..HEAD window) added the mention-reachable fixture and shifted this test down; addresses re-read at HEAD, so they carry.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper — the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at d1af9a5 and holds — nothing ruled the hop semantics; VERIFIER-TYPED's window touched graph.rs but not the const region (still 5 at graph.rs:59, cite still 2026-07-02 at 55-58). The park's subject is untouched, and may still be wrong. A build-side friction capture records the same disagreement; the human's to read, not plan's to drain. rescoped 4a33911.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at d1af9a5, holding on every clause: no v0.1 tag (only era tags), crate 0.1.0, release.yml still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. `git diff 68fae5c..HEAD -- .github/` is empty. scoped b676656.

summary: main.rs's seven admissibility/collision judges share one job — declared-program well-formedness — and move together to a new judges module admissibility

new.description: New flat module, judges subsystem (architecture.md's codemap: 'evaluation over the resolved corpus') — human-ruled (inbox, observed b4a6947): the seven admissibility/collision-diagnostic functions cluster by one job, declared-program well-formedness (drift::Declarations in, check::Diagnostic out), not by which of engine.rs/graph.rs they happen to call (only joined_kind_admissibility calls engine::admissibility — a judges-internal import, no seam per the ruling; the other six call neither). Moved verbatim (current HEAD line numbers, reverify at pickup): joined_kind_admissibility (1740-1755), satisfies_label_admissibility (2036-2067) with SATISFIES_LABEL_ADMISSIBILITY_RULE (2028) and NESTED_MEMBER_ADMISSIBILITY_RULE (2025), nested_member_admissibility (2076-2095), kind_collision_diagnostic (2265-2278) with KIND_COLLISION_RULE (2210), clause_collision_diagnostics (2301-2332) with CLAUSE_COLLISION_RULE (2283), local_locus_admissibility (2343-2369) with LOCAL_LOCUS_RULE (2338), governs_collision_diagnostics (2396-2435) with its own governs_collision_diagnostic singular helper (2439-2454) and GOVERNS_COLLISION_RULE (2375). Also carries declared_embedded_kinds (2000-2020), nested_member_admissibility's set builder: forced out of main.rs alongside it (a library module cannot call back into the main.rs binary crate) — its other caller, embedded_features_by_kind, stays in main.rs until MAIN-GATE-VERB-MODULE-EXTRACT moves it and calls admissibility::declared_embedded_kinds meanwhile (main.rs calling a library function is unproblematic); build may instead judge declared_embedded_kinds belongs in compose.rs beside embedded_features_by_kind's eventual home, with admissibility.rs calling compose:: — judges consuming model is the directionality the codemap already establishes, so either placement is architecturally sound. Name the choice in the commit body. local_locus_admissibility and governs_collision_diagnostics both call overlay_builtin_kind, already forced out of main.rs by MAIN-CORPUS-ASSEMBLY-TO-COMPOSE's own move (resolve_kind_units/kind_features become its callers) — call it at whichever qualified path that entry lands, most likely compose::overlay_builtin_kind.

edit.description: Delete the seven moved functions, their six RULE consts, governs_collision_diagnostic, and declared_embedded_kinds (whichever module wins). gate()'s calls requalify to admissibility::: clause_collision_diagnostics (910), kind_collision_diagnostic (952), governs_collision_diagnostics (956), local_locus_admissibility (964), nested_member_admissibility (1020), joined_kind_admissibility (1055), satisfies_label_admissibility (1061).

notes: Item 3 of inbox RULED (session, standing delegation, observed b4a6947): unparks MAIN-JUDGE-VERB-HOME-RULING's first half — clustering by job (well-formedness), not by engine/graph call shape. Serialized behind MAIN-CORPUS-ASSEMBLY-TO-COMPOSE (shared main.rs); declared_embedded_kinds/overlay_builtin_kind's exact landing module is that entry's or this one's build-time call. scoped at 8104308.

summary: gate's dispatch body (839-1214) becomes a new flat verbs module gate — main.rs merely calls it, the install/bundle precedent for a verb's body

new.description: New flat module, verbs subsystem (architecture.md's codemap: main/install/bundle — 'main carries dispatch only; corpus assembly and judgment live in the library'). Carries gate's dispatch body verbatim (839-1214, current HEAD, reverify at pickup) as `pub fn gate`. Hard constraint, not a style choice: gate.rs is a library module while gate() lives in the main.rs binary crate today, so every main.rs-private helper gate() calls must also move into the library, or gate.rs cannot compile. Verified via rg (current HEAD): kind_selections (1896-1911, gate's sole caller) and mode_from_declarations (644-666, gate's own doc names gate as its reason to exist; also called again at main.rs:384, the CLI dispatch, which keeps calling it fine post-move) default into gate.rs directly. partition_kind_rows (2246-2260, plus its sole-caller helper row_relocates_builtin, 2224-2234), repo_file_set (1820-1831), embedded_features_by_kind (1928-1951), and collect_directive_members (2171-2202) are each ALSO called by explain() (main.rs:554/606/575/607 respectively), which stays in main.rs until MAIN-EXPLAIN-READ-CONSOLIDATE ships next — main.rs keeps calling library functions fine meanwhile, so these four just need one shared home reachable from gate.rs and (for now) main.rs. compose.rs is the well-argued default (member composition, the job MAIN-CORPUS-ASSEMBLY-TO-COMPOSE already establishes there; partition_kind_rows's own doc comment already names explain/gate/collect_directive_members as its three call sites). Exact split is a judgment call for build, named in the commit body — if any land in compose.rs instead of gate.rs, that file is edited too.

edit.description: Delete gate()'s body (839-1214) and whichever helpers travel per the above. harness_diagnostics (821-829, its three call sites) and the inline test (~2651) requalify to gate::gate(...); the top-level Command dispatch's mode_from_declarations call (384) requalifies to wherever that helper lands. explain()'s still-resident call sites (554, 575, 606, 607) requalify to the four shared helpers' landed qualifier — call-site-only, explain's own body is untouched until the next entry.
Add `pub mod gate;` alongside the existing `pub mod install;`/`pub mod bundle;` — the verbs subsystem's new library entry.

notes: Item 3 of inbox RULED (session, standing delegation, observed b4a6947): completes MAIN-JUDGE-VERB-HOME-RULING's second half, explain's piece — read.rs already IS the read-resolution home, no new module. Serialized behind MAIN-GATE-VERB-MODULE-EXTRACT (shared main.rs); the read::explain name collision is a real, disk-verified fact, not a hypothetical. scoped at 8104308.

summary: CollectionAddress gains a closed entry-shape fact (object/scalar/group-array) as kind data; ENABLEMENT_FIELD's field-name half becomes the enabledPlugins kind's own declared shape (decision 0042)

edit.description: `CollectionAddress` (184-190, two fields: `manifest`, `key_path: CollectionKeyPath`) gains a third field carrying decision 0042's closed entry-shape fact: `object` (the value's keys are the member's fields, today's unmarked default), `scalar` (the value is the member's one declared field, carrying that field's name — this is where `ENABLEMENT_FIELD`'s (219, `pub(crate) const ENABLEMENT_FIELD: &str = "enabled"`) knowledge relocates), and `group-array` (the value is an array of groups, each carrying lifted shared fields plus a member array — the member-array key and lifted field names are its own parameters, settled from the format docs `claude_code_hook`'s doc comment already cites). Exact Rust shape (an `EntryShape` enum with data-carrying variants, `Scalar { field: String }`/`GroupArray { member_key: String, lifted_fields: Vec<String> }`) is a judgment call for build, named in the commit body. `CollectionKeyPath` (196-302, its four variants and `collection_key`/`key_field`/`identity_edge`/`spans_whole_manifest` methods) is UNCHANGED — it still names the manifest's top-level key; only the identity-branching *consumers* of it (a later chain entry) retire, not the type. `collection_address_from_row` (1155-1169) decodes the new row column into the new field; `ENABLEMENT_FIELD` itself is NOT deleted here — it retires only once its last two consumers (json_manifest.rs, graph.rs — later chain entries) are gone.

The four shipped `CollectionAddress { .. }` literals declare their real shape, each already carrying the citation this fact draws from in its surrounding doc comment: `claude_code_hook` (238-241, doc 225-230, `code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks`, retrieved 2026-07-16) declares `group-array`; `claude_code_mcp_server` (265-268, doc 254-259, 'a server entry is an object') declares `object`; `claude_code_installed_plugin` (302-305) declares `scalar` with field `"enabled"` (retiring what `ENABLEMENT_FIELD` encoded); `claude_code_known_marketplace` (343-346, doc: 'Its value is an object, unlike an installed plugin's bare boolean') declares `object`. The two in-module test literals (835, 872) gain the matching field.

`CollectionAddressRow` (2893-2900, `manifest`/`key_path` only) gains the wire column carrying the new fact; `collection_address_table`/`collection_address_from_table` (3806-3813/3815-3823) read/write it. `src/builtin_lock.toml`'s four registration rows (approx lines 106/115/124/140 — hook/mcp-server/installed-plugin/known-marketplace) gain matching real values, since `KindFactRow` decodes only `Deserialize` (no default) and the embedded lock is every bare harness's default-contract source (`.flume/plan/open-questions.md`, 'Default-contract auto-adoption').
`CollectionAddress` interface (137-144, `manifest`/`keyPath` only) gains the mirrored `entryShape`-shaped fact, matching whichever Rust shape build lands.
`collectionAddressRow` (235-238) lowers the new fact into the row's new wire column, alongside the existing `manifest`/`key_path` mapping.
Line 19/57-60's `CollectionAddress { .. }` assertion against `definition("hook")` gains the group-array shape's expected value.
Line 22/53-56's `CollectionAddress { .. }` assertion gains the object shape's expected value.
Line 24/96-99's `CollectionAddress { .. }` assertion gains the scalar shape's expected value (field `"enabled"`); the lock-row read at 217-234 gains the new column's raw-wire assertion.
Line 26/73-76's `CollectionAddress { .. }` assertion gains the object shape's expected value.
The `mcp_address()` helper (17, used at 23-27 and across the file) constructs a bare `CollectionAddress` directly — gains the new field (object shape, matching `mcp-server`'s real declaration).

`a_hook_round_trips_settings_json_read_to_members_to_write_byte_identical` (924, `CollectionAddress { .. }` at 960-963) gains the group-array shape's expected value — the round trip itself is unaffected, this entry adds no dispatch change.

`a_kinds_fields_only_shape_and_collection_address_round_trip_the_lock_and_reach_the_engine` (1165-1207) constructs `CollectionAddress { .. }` directly to assert `CustomKind::from_kind_fact_row`'s decode output — gains the new field on both sides of the round trip.

schemaDelta: CollectionAddressRow (src/drift.rs, ts-rs-mirrored to sdk/src/generated/CollectionAddressRow.ts) gains a new column carrying the closed entry-shape fact; exact wire shape is a judgment call for build, named in the commit body. Regenerates via the existing ts-rs export test — never hand-edit the generated file.

tests.asserts: the new entry-shape column round-trips losslessly through emit + read_declarations for all three shape kinds (object/scalar/group-array), the same bar CONTRACT-REQUIRE-SECTIONS-ROUNDTRIP's precedent sets
the embedded lock's frozen-snapshot test is re-recorded (not silently stale) to include the four registration rows' new column — grep this file's own re-record discipline at pickup

acceptance: CollectionAddress carries a closed entry-shape fact as kind data (object/scalar/group-array); the four shipped registration kinds declare their real shape with the citations they already carry; the lock row and SDK mirror round-trip it losslessly. Nothing dispatches on it yet — this entry is purely additive, no behavior change. cargo test, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and pnpm --dir sdk test all green.
notes: Derived from decision 0042, Consequences bullets 1, 3, 4. kind.rs/drift.rs cites corrected for line shifts KIND-ZERO-CONSUMER-EXPORTS-PRUNE and DRIFT-SOURCE-DEP-PARSE-HOIST left (3561798). Unblocked: IMPORT-ROLLUP-WRITER-PLACEMENT shipped ab2e822. Reserialized behind MAIN-LOCK-ROW-CONSTRUCTORS-TO-DRIFT, last chain entry on drift.rs. Still conflicts with open TAP-PAYLOAD-SCHEMA-SPLIT over builtin_kind.rs — single-tag blockedBy can't express both waits; re-check at either's ship. rescoped 863e506.

summary: manifest_members and emit's write faces dispatch on the collection's declared entry-shape instead of CollectionKeyPath identity; the HooksEvent/EnabledPlugins branches delete (decision 0042)

per.section: The fix lands at the mechanism

edit.description: `manifest_members` (538-565) branches on collection *identity* — `if collection_key == CollectionKeyPath::HooksEvent.collection_key()` (545) and `::EnabledPlugins.collection_key()` (555) — with the object-shape read (`entry_fields`, 571-579) as the unconditional fallthrough tail (561-564). Re-key the dispatch to match on the resolved collection's declared entry-shape (from KIND-ENTRY-SHAPE-DATA-DECLARE) instead: `Scalar`/`GroupArray`/`Object`, deleting both identity comparisons. `enablement_member_fields` (598-603) and `enablement_entry_value` (615-620, `pub(crate)`) stop reading the bare `ENABLEMENT_FIELD` constant and take the shape's own field name as a parameter instead. `hook_member_fields` (630-658) and `hook_matcher_group` (667-686, `pub(crate)`) stop hardcoding the literal strings `"matcher"`/`"hooks"` and take the shape's own member-array key / lifted field names as parameters instead. `entry_fields` (object-shape read) has no named write-side inverse today — its write is inlined in `drift.rs`'s `emit` (see below); promote it into a first-class function beside its siblings for symmetry, or leave the inline and only change its dispatch condition — a judgment call for build, named in the commit body.

`emit`'s registration-write loop (1006-1058) branches on the identical identity pair: `if collection == CollectionKeyPath::HooksEvent.collection_key()` (1028) and `::EnabledPlugins.collection_key()` (1040), with the object-shape write inlined unconditionally in the `else` (1049-1057). Re-key to dispatch on the registration's collection's declared entry-shape, calling `json_manifest`'s now-shape-parameterized write functions (and the object-shape write, wherever it now lives per the json_manifest.rs judgment call) instead of the identity `if`/`else if`/`else` chain.

tests.asserts: enablement_members_carry_the_scalar_value_and_re_render_identically (~970-1007), an_enablement_member_with_no_enabled_field_renders_the_documented_true (~1009-1014), and hook_members_decompose_the_matcher_group_array_and_re_nest_identically (~1016-1051) all pass calling the now-parameterized functions — identical behavior, dispatch-only rewrite
a_hook_round_trips_settings_json_read_to_members_to_write_byte_identical (924) stays byte-identical through the rekeyed dispatch

composition 3 (116-126: sessionHook/formatterPlugin/acmeMarketplace — group-array/scalar/object composed in one settings.json) stays snapshot-identical, proving the rekeyed dispatch handles all three shapes together in one manifest — this already-existing composition discharges decision 0042's 'gauntlet gains a fixture composing the three shapes' consequence (verified on disk this tick, no new fixture needed)

acceptance: manifest_members and emit's registration-write loop both dispatch on the collection's declared entry-shape; no CollectionKeyPath identity comparison remains in either function. Byte-identical output to today (engineering.md, 'The fix lands at the mechanism': the special case generalizes, the case stops being special). cargo test and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings green.
notes: Derived from decision 0042, Consequences bullet 2 (json_manifest.rs half) plus the drift.rs write-face half the decision's own text folds into 'the write faces re-key.' Bullet 5 (gauntlet fixture) verified-already-moot on disk this tick via tests/gauntlet.rs:116-126 — see tests[] above.

summary: graph.rs's dead-plugin judge stops reading the bare ENABLEMENT_FIELD constant; kind.rs deletes it once both consumers are gone (decision 0042's retirement, completed)

per.section: The fix lands at the mechanism

edit.description: `dead_registration`'s `Registration::Enablement` arm (727-739, reading `crate::kind::ENABLEMENT_FIELD` at 728 and 737) is the constant's last consumer once json_manifest.rs's own two uses (JSON-MANIFEST-ENTRY-SHAPE-DISPATCH) are gone. `dead_registration` receives only `&Registration`/`&Features` — no `CollectionAddress`/kind data reaches this call site today (traced: `dead_channel_set` → `reachable`/`live_members`, both keyed by bare kind name off `main.rs:591-596`'s `registrations: BTreeMap<&str, Vec<Registration>>`, which drops `CustomKind::collection_address` when building the map). Two designs, a judgment call for build, named in the commit body: (a) widen `Registration::Enablement` to carry its own `field: String`, mirroring the sibling variants `DescriptionTrigger`/`PathsMatch`/`Event`'s existing per-variant field, populated from the enabled-plugins kind's declared `Scalar { field }` shape when `main.rs` builds the registration map; or (b) thread `CollectionAddress`/entry-shape data down through `reachable`/`live_members`/`dead_channel_set`/`dead_registration`'s signatures. Once resolved, delete `kind.rs`'s now-fully-unused `ENABLEMENT_FIELD` const (219, doc 211-218).

If design (a) is chosen: the registration-map construction (591-596) populates `Registration::Enablement`'s new `field` from the enabled-plugins kind's declared shape when building `registrations`. If design (b): the map (or a parallel one) carries `CollectionAddress` data through to `graph::reachable`'s call site — exact threading is the same build-time judgment call as the graph.rs edit.

tests.asserts: a_false_valued_entry_gates_its_member_off_every_channel (154) stays green — the disabled plugin is still found dead, the two enabled ones still live, unchanged behavior through whichever design lands

existing dead_registration inline tests (e.g. a_paths_match_glob_dies_only_when_no_repo_file_matches_it and its neighbors) stay green — the retirement is signature-only for the Enablement arm, no change to other channels

acceptance: graph.rs's dead-plugin judge reads the field name from kind data, never the bare ENABLEMENT_FIELD constant; kind.rs no longer declares it. cargo test and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings green.
notes: Derived from decision 0042, completing Consequences bullet 1's ENABLEMENT_FIELD retirement (graph.rs was not named in the decision's own text, which lists only kind.rs/json_manifest.rs — but ENABLEMENT_FIELD cannot retire while this file still reads it; the retirement's completion, not new scope). Last entry in the 0042 chain. scoped at efe64aa.

summary: builtin_kind.rs's test module carries a private surface_unit byte-identical to tests/common's, instead of the crate's own designated shared-fixture home, test_support.rs

edit.description: The inline `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (570) declares a private `fn surface_unit(member: &crate::frontmatter::Member) -> Unit` (888-900, doc 885-887 'Lift an imported ... straight into the raw Unit the composed extractor reads', closing brace 901) that is a byte-for-byte body duplicate — and near-identical doc comment — of `tests/common::surface_unit` (`tests/common/mod.rs`, pub fn at 463-476, doc 460-462, same wording). This same test module already imports `crate::test_support::tmpdir` (574) — the crate's own designated home for in-src `#[cfg(test)]` fixture scaffolding (`src/test_support.rs`'s own header: 'Shared scaffolding for in-src `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` blocks'), so the pattern for sharing this exact class of helper is already established and simply wasn't used for this one. Delete the private `surface_unit` (885-901); add `crate::test_support::surface_unit` to the existing `use crate::test_support::tmpdir;` import line (or a neighboring one), and leave its five call sites (933, 984, 1013, 1027, 1046) unchanged — same name, same signature, now resolved from the shared home.

Add `pub(crate) fn surface_unit(member: &crate::frontmatter::Member) -> Unit { .. }` here, lifted verbatim from builtin_kind.rs's deleted copy (same field-by-field construction: id, frontmatter, body, source_path, satisfies, satisfies_clauses). Add `crate::kind::Unit` to this file's existing `use crate::kind::{CustomKind, Extraction, Format, Governs, Primitive, UnitShape};` import line; `crate::frontmatter::Member` stays referenced by its qualified path in the signature, as the deleted original did, needing no new import.

tests.asserts: skill_features_fold_unknown_keys_and_surface_satisfies_off_the_surface and its four sibling tests calling surface_unit (984, 1013, 1027, 1046) stay green — the move is location-only, identical Unit construction

acceptance: builtin_kind.rs's test module has no private surface_unit; it calls crate::test_support::surface_unit, the same shared home the module already draws tmpdir from. src/test_support.rs is the fn's one implementation for in-src unit tests. cargo test and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings green.
notes: Found sweeping provider (posture rotation: judges skip-forwarded since fe3ff3f, provider touched by 404b73a/516f8f6). tests/common::surface_unit stays untouched — test_support.rs is pub(crate) (lib.rs:58), a different compilation unit, not a three-way merge. Serialized behind KIND-ENTRY-SHAPE-DATA-DECLARE, sharing builtin_kind.rs; TAP-PAYLOAD-SCHEMA-SPLIT also touches it, open/queue-front, ships first regardless. scoped at e5d01fe.

summary: kind.rs's self-contained layout-document reader (Layout family, 284-565, needs only extract) extracts to a new flat layout module — human-ruled split, the algebra stays whole

new.description: New flat module, model subsystem (architecture.md's codemap) — the layout-document reader moved verbatim from kind.rs: Layout (struct, 284-287), LayoutRegion (291-313), LayoutReading (322-337), LayoutMember (343-356), LayoutError (362-389), impl Layout (396-479, the read method), next_heading (484-491), read_collection_member (497-525), parse_edge_entries (532-538), slugify (544-559). Verified: depends only on crate::extract and std path/collection types, no CustomKind reference anywhere in the moved body. SATISFIES_EDGE_FIELD (kind.rs 394) stays behind — textually inside the moved range but logically CustomKind's own edge-slot vocabulary (used by CustomKind::edge_slots at kind.rs 894/904, and externally by drift.rs/main.rs), never read by Layout's own methods — layout.rs imports it from crate::kind rather than owning a copy.

edit.description: Delete the moved Layout family (284-390, 396-565 — SATISFIES_EDGE_FIELD at 394 is the one line in that span that stays). Content's Layout variant (141+) now wraps crate::layout::Layout.
Imports Layout/LayoutRegion from crate::kind (line 30) and uses them at 1095, 1701, 1715, 1758-1761 — requalify every reference to crate::layout, no re-export shim in kind.rs.
References kind::Layout (1269), kind::LayoutError (1262 doc), kind::Content::Layout (1434, 1782) — requalify the Layout/LayoutError references to crate::layout.

tests.asserts: existing layout-document read/gate coverage stays green — the move is location-only, no read-behavior change
edge-field parsing (satisfies among them) stays green — SATISFIES_EDGE_FIELD's cross-module wiring (layout.rs importing kind.rs's const) resolves identically

acceptance: src/layout.rs exists in the model subsystem carrying the Layout family verbatim; kind.rs no longer defines Layout/LayoutRegion/LayoutReading/LayoutMember/LayoutError/next_heading/read_collection_member/parse_edge_entries/slugify. cargo test and cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings green.
notes: Human-ruled via inbox (600ffd5, kind-cohesion capture): layout reader → new flat layout module. MAIN-IMPORT-EDGES-DOC-PARAM-COLLAPSE shipped (cb4f989) — unblocked, but collides on main.rs with the now-open GRAPH-RESOLVED-EDGE-WALK-CONSOLIDATE; reserialized behind it. Also shares drift.rs with the now-open DRIFT-EMIT-OUTCOME-LABEL-ZERO-CONSUMER-PRUNE — single-tag blockedBy can't express both waits; the graph entry is queue-front, ships first regardless. scoped at 612f2a5.

summary: kind.rs's crate-wide glob-compilation cache (compile_glob/GLOB_CACHE/pins, 1223-1284) extracts to a new flat foundation module glob — human-ruled split, three external callers by its own doc

new.description: New flat module, foundation subsystem (architecture.md's codemap: leaf vocabulary, globset-only, no harness knowledge) — compile_glob (1236-1259, doc 1223-1234), the GLOB_CACHE/GLOB_COMPILES thread_locals (1261-1274), and glob_compile_count (1281-1283, doc 1276-1280), moved verbatim from kind.rs. compile_glob's own doc comment names its callers: import's discovery walk, coverage_note's governs leaf test, graph's paths-match liveness test, plus kind.rs's own internal use (owns_source, 952; Primitive::matches, 1503-1507).

edit.description: Delete the moved glob-cache block (1223-1284); requalify internal callers (owns_source's governs-glob match, 952; Primitive::matches, 1503-1507) to crate::glob::compile_glob.
Call site (447, crate::kind::compile_glob) requalifies to crate::glob::compile_glob.
Call site (946, crate::kind::compile_glob) and its doc comment naming the surface (933) requalify to crate::glob::compile_glob.
Call site (752) and its doc comment naming the surface (1932) requalify to crate::glob::compile_glob.
The import (26, use crate::kind::{CustomKind, compile_glob}) and three call sites (355, 363, 380) requalify to crate::glob::compile_glob.
Two call sites (147, 153, kind::glob_compile_count) requalify to glob::glob_compile_count — the count-pin itself is unaffected, location-only.

notes: Human-ruled via inbox (600ffd5, kind-cohesion capture, superseding this tick's own first-pass parked filing): glob cache → new flat foundation module (glob) — globset-only, knows no harness. Serialized behind KIND-LAYOUT-READER-MODULE-EXTRACT (shared kind.rs); also shares import.rs/coverage_note.rs with open IMPORT-ROLLUP-WRITER-PLACEMENT/COVERAGE-KNOWN-SURFACES-RELOCATE, both queue-earlier and ship first. scoped at 600ffd5.
